name: Río Segura Trail (GR 127)
name_original: Paseo Ribereño de Cieza a PR-MU 11 Medina Siyasa — circular desde Cieza
description: Jog the moderate 6.3-mile Río Segura Trail (GR 127) in Cieza, enjoying riverside views and a 394-foot elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging around Cieza offers diverse landscapes, with routes along the Segura River and through surrounding mountain ranges. The region features a mix of riverside paths, agricultural areas, and elevated trails. Terrain varies from mostly paved surfaces to more challenging mountain paths with significant elevation changes. Best jogging routes around Cieza The most popular jogging route is Return from Cieza, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 4 minutes to complete. This moderate route primarily follows paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local runners is Río Segura Trail (GR 127), a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This route offers scenic riverside views along the Segura River. Local runners also love the Sierra de Ascoy and GR 7 Loop, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Ascoy, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Jogging around Cieza is defined by the Segura River, the Sierra de Ascoy, and blooming orchards, with options for both easy riverside runs and more challenging mountain trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Cieza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Cieza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cieza?
A: Cieza offers a diverse selection of over 30 running routes. These include 1 easy route, 19 moderate routes, and 12 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Cieza?
A: The running routes in Cieza feature a varied terrain. You'll find paths along the Segura River, through agricultural areas, and up into the surrounding mountain ranges. Terrain can range from mostly paved surfaces, ideal for a steady jog, to more challenging mountain paths with significant elevation changes for experienced runners.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Cieza?
A: Yes, Cieza has routes suitable for beginners. While there is 1 officially easy route, many of the 19 moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375198368/return-from-cieza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return from Cieza&lt;/a&gt;, are primarily on paved surfaces and offer a good starting point for those new to running in the area. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375226917/playa-fluvial-la-presa-desde-cieza-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa fluvial "La Presa" desde Cieza — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is also mostly paved.

Q: Are there challenging running routes for experienced runners in Cieza?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Cieza offers 12 difficult routes. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374379759/sierra-de-ascoy-and-gr-7-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Ascoy and GR 7 Loop&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375215105/medina-siyasa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina Siyâsa Trail&lt;/a&gt; provide significant elevation gains and demanding terrain through the Sierra de Ascoy, offering panoramic views and a rewarding workout.

Q: What natural features can I enjoy while running in Cieza?
A: Cieza's running routes are rich in natural beauty. You can jog along the tranquil Segura River, explore the stunning Almadenes Canyon, or witness the spectacular blooming orchards during 'La Floración'. Mountain ranges like the Sierra de Ascoy and Sierra de Ricote offer dramatic backdrops, while the Alfonso XIII Reservoir and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5051429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia de la Andelma&lt;/a&gt; provide unique sights.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the running routes?
A: Yes, several routes integrate historical and cultural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375215105/medina-siyasa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina Siyâsa Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads you past the significant Islamic archaeological site of Medina Siyâsa. You might also encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5051429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia de la Andelma&lt;/a&gt;, an irrigation channel with Roman and Arab origins.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Cieza?
A: The best time to go running in Cieza is typically from mid-February to mid-March, during the spectacular 'La Floración'. This is when the peach, plum, almond, apricot, and nectarine orchards burst into bloom, transforming the landscape into a vibrant tapestry of colors. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cieza?
A: Yes, Cieza offers several circular running routes. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375226917/playa-fluvial-la-presa-desde-cieza-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa fluvial "La Presa" desde Cieza — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a convenient loop for runners. Many other routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Cieza?
A: The running experience in Cieza is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from tranquil riverside paths along the Segura River to challenging mountain trails with expansive views, and the unique beauty of the blooming orchards during spring.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic viewpoints in Cieza?
A: Yes, Cieza's mountainous terrain provides excellent opportunities for panoramic views. Routes through the Sierra de Ascoy, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374379759/sierra-de-ascoy-and-gr-7-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Ascoy and GR 7 Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer expansive vistas. You can also find viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Cross of Abarán&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3044804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Azud de Ojós and Ricote&lt;/a&gt;, which provide stunning perspectives of the valley and river.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Segura River?
A: Yes, the Segura River is a central feature of Cieza's natural beauty, and many running routes follow its course. A popular option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374094262/rio-segura-trail-gr-127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Segura Trail (GR 127)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic riverside views and tranquil paths along the water.
tips: Beautiful picture of the Segura river as it passes through Cieza from the Iron Bridge.
This is a flat, easy trail with great views of the river and surrounding mountains. Perfect for a quick getaway out of the city.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of jogging this section of the Río Segura Trail?
faqs.answer: This specific section of the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) is rated as moderate for jogging. While many parts of the GR 127 are generally easy, this particular route offers a balanced challenge suitable for those with some jogging experience. The overall GR 127 is a long-distance trail, and its difficulty varies significantly across its many stages.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain, typical of riverside paths. The majority of the GR 127 follows riverbanks, often featuring paths, tracks, and roads that are generally easy to navigate. This section will likely include a mix of these surfaces, potentially with some paved areas and riverside vegetation. Be aware that the broader GR 127 has sections like the Cañón de Almadenes which are much more challenging, but this specific route is designed for jogging.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along this route?
faqs.answer: This route offers beautiful views along the Segura River. You'll get a great View of the Segura River from Puente de Hierro and enjoy the tranquil Path Along the Segura River – Cieza. The broader GR 127 also features diverse landscapes like the Ricote Valley, rice fields of Calasparra, and historical sites.

faqs.question: Is this section of the Río Segura Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating, this specific jogging route is suitable for beginners who are already comfortable with short to medium distances. The generally gentle inclines along the river make it accessible, but its length and duration mean a basic level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This particular route, the Ribereno Walk from Cieza, is a circular trail. This means you will start and end at the same point, offering a convenient jogging experience without needing to arrange transport back to your starting location.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route is described as 'circular from Cieza', suggesting that Cieza is a primary access point. You should look for public parking areas within Cieza that are convenient to the river path. Specific parking details are not provided, but Cieza is a town with various access points to the river.

faqs.question: Is the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, riverside paths in Spain are dog-friendly, but local regulations can vary. It's advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in more populated areas or near wildlife. Always carry waste bags and ensure your dog is well-behaved around other users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Murcia region can get very hot in summer. The best times to jog this route are typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and more pleasant. Winter can also be suitable, though mornings might be chilly. Avoid peak summer heat, especially midday.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: No, generally, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and jog on the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) or its sections. It is a public trail system.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the broader Río Segura Trail (GR 127) intersects with and sometimes follows sections of other official trails. Along its course, you might encounter parts of the Río Segura, Transmurciana 22, Murcia, Medina Siyasa (Cieza), and Paseo Ribereño trails.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging session on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a comfortable jogging experience, bring appropriate athletic footwear, comfortable clothing suitable for the weather, and sufficient water, especially during warmer months. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also recommended. While the route is circular, a basic map or GPS device can be helpful for navigation.
